title: Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Authentic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get creamy, rich, and flavorful results at home. Perfect for lunch!

lowCompetitionKeywords: homemade Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani recipe
seo.h1: Authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
h2Headings: What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and a side of naan bread.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, a classic Indian Murgh Makhani dish perfect for any meal.

description: Butter Chicken, also known as Murgh Makhani, is a beloved Indian curry featuring tender chicken pieces bathed in a rich, creamy tomato-based sauce. This iconic dish is celebrated for its mild spices, buttery texture, and a subtle sweetness that makes it a favorite across the globe. Perfect for a comforting and flavorful Indian lunch or dinner.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 min

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, making sure not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add another 2 tablespoons of butter. Add chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer and Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, soaked cashews, and sugar. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove from heat, discard bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if you can find them easily). Carefully transfer the mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it is too thick.

instructions.title: Finish the Butter Chicken Sauce
instructions.text: Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Stir in the cooked chicken. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter and the crushed kasoori methi. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the sauce.

instructions.title: Add Cream and Serve
instructions.text: Finally, stir in the heavy cream and heat through gently for 1-2 minutes without boiling. Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan bread, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For a deeper flavor, char the marinated chicken slightly in a tandoor or under a broiler before adding to the sauce.
Soaking cashews is crucial for a smooth, rich sauce. If you dont have cashews, blanched almonds can be a good substitute.
Always add heavy cream at the very end and avoid boiling the sauce afterwards to prevent it from splitting.
article:
Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is more than just a dish; it's a culinary hug, a vibrant symphony of flavors that has captivated palates worldwide. Originating from the bustling streets of Delhi, this iconic Indian lunch recipe has earned its place as a global favorite, renowned for its incredibly tender chicken enveloped in a rich, creamy, and mildly spiced tomato-based gravy. Whether you're a seasoned chef or a home cook looking to explore the depths of Indian cuisine, mastering Butter Chicken promises a rewarding experience. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know to create this delightful dish right in your own kitchen, transforming your lunch into an extraordinary feast.

## What is Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani?
At its heart, Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani as it's known in Hindi (literally meaning "chicken with butter"), is a classic North Indian curry. Its origins trace back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi in the 1950s, where enterprising chefs combined leftover tandoori chicken with a luscious tomato and butter gravy. The result was an instant sensation. The dish features succulent pieces of chicken, traditionally marinated in yogurt and spices before being grilled or pan-fried, then simmered in a velvety sauce made from tomatoes, butter, fresh cream, cashews, and a delicate blend of aromatic spices like garam masala and fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). The distinct balance of sweet, tangy, and savory flavors, coupled with its luxurious texture, makes Butter Chicken a truly unforgettable meal. It’s the ultimate comfort food, perfect for a cozy lunch or a celebratory dinner.

## Ingredients You Need
Creating an authentic Butter Chicken requires a few key components, each playing a crucial role in building its signature flavor profile.

**For the Chicken Marinade:**
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder
*   1/2 teaspoon turmeric powder
*   1 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

**For the Makhani Gravy:**
*   2 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1 tablespoon oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tablespoon ginger-garlic paste
*   1 cup tomato puree (from ripe tomatoes or canned diced tomatoes blended smooth)
*   1/4 cup cashews, soaked in warm water for 15 minutes (or 2 tablespoons almond flour for nut-free)
*   1 teaspoon red chili powder (adjust to your spice preference)
*   1 teaspoon coriander powder
*   1/2 teaspoon cumin powder
*   1/2 teaspoon garam masala
*   1/4 teaspoon cardamom powder (optional, but highly recommended)
*   1 teaspoon kasoori methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   1/2 cup heavy cream
*   1-2 tablespoons sugar (to balance acidity)
*   Salt to taste
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   Fresh coriander leaves for garnish

## Step-by-Step Instructions
Follow these steps to craft a perfect Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani:

**1. Marinate the Chicken:** In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with all the marinade ingredients. Mix well to ensure the chicken is evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally for 2-4 hours, to allow the flavors to penetrate and tenderize the chicken.

**2. Cook the Chicken:** Heat a tablespoon of oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until the chicken is browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. You can also grill or bake the chicken for a more authentic tandoor-like flavor. Once cooked, set the chicken aside.

**3. Prepare the Gravy Base:** In the same pan (or a separate heavy-bottomed pot), melt the butter with the remaining oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onions and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

**4. Build the Gravy Flavors:** Pour in the tomato puree. Add the soaked cashews (drained), red chili powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Mix well and cook, stirring occasionally, for 8-10 minutes, until the tomato mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate from the sides. This step is crucial for developing a deep flavor.

**5. Blend for Smoothness:** Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Carefully transfer the entire mixture to a blender and blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky gravy, pass the blended mixture through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot, pressing down on any solids. Discard the solids.

**6. Finish the Gravy:** Return the smooth gravy to medium heat. Stir in the garam masala, cardamom powder (if using), and kasoori methi. Add water or chicken broth to achieve your desired consistency. Bring to a gentle simmer.

**7. Combine and Serve:** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering gravy. Stir in the heavy cream and sugar. Taste and adjust salt and sugar as needed. Simmer for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the rich flavors of the gravy. Garnish generously with fresh coriander leaves before serving.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results
Achieving restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tricks:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be. Overnight marination in the fridge yields the best results.
*   **Charred Chicken Adds Depth:** If possible, lightly char the chicken either by grilling or pan-frying it until it has a slight smoky char. This mimics the tandoor oven effect and adds incredible depth to the Butter Chicken.
*   **Blend and Strain:** For the silkiest, most luxurious gravy, always blend the cooked gravy base until perfectly smooth. Straining it through a fine-mesh sieve removes any tiny bits of onion or cashew, resulting in an incredibly smooth texture.
*   **Use Quality Ingredients:** Good quality butter, fresh cream, and ripe tomatoes make a significant difference in the final taste of your Murgh Makhani.
*   **Don't Skip Kasoori Methi:** Dried fenugreek leaves are non-negotiable for authentic Butter Chicken flavor. Crush them between your palms before adding to release their aromatic oils.
*   **Balance Sweet and Tangy:** The sugar is essential to balance the acidity of the tomatoes and enhance the overall flavor profile. Adjust to your preference.

## Serving Suggestions
Butter Chicken is incredibly versatile and pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice dishes. For a truly authentic experience, serve it piping hot with:

*   **Naan Bread:** Garlic naan or butter naan are perfect for soaking up every last drop of the rich gravy.
*   **Basmati Rice:** Plain steamed basmati rice or fragrant jeera (cumin) rice provides a gentle contrast to the curry's robust flavors.
*   **Roti or Paratha:** These unleavened Indian flatbreads are also excellent accompaniments.
*   **Garnish:** A final swirl of fresh cream and a sprinkle of chopped fresh coriander leaves not only adds visual appeal but also enhances the freshness of the dish.
*   **Sides:** A simple side salad of sliced onions, cucumber, and lemon wedges, or a cooling raita (yogurt dip) can complement the richness of the curry.

## Variations and Substitutions
While the classic Butter Chicken is a beloved masterpiece, there are many ways to adapt this recipe to suit different dietary needs or preferences:

*   **Vegetarian Butter Chicken:** For a delicious vegetarian twist, replace the chicken with paneer (Indian cottage cheese), tofu, or a medley of vegetables like cauliflower, peas, and potatoes. Simply pan-fry or bake the paneer/tofu before adding it to the gravy.
*   **Vegan Murgh Makhani:** This can be achieved by using plant-based butter, coconut cream or a cashew-based cream substitute instead of dairy cream, and plant-based protein like marinated tofu or seitan.
*   **Healthier Options:** To reduce the calorie count, you can use less butter and cream, opt for low-fat yogurt in the marinade, and bake or air-fry the chicken instead of pan-frying.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your liking. For a spicier kick, you can add a finely chopped green chili along with the ginger-garlic paste.
*   **Nut-Free Gravy:** If you have nut allergies, you can omit the cashews. To maintain a thick consistency, a small amount of cornstarch slurry (1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water) can be added to the simmering gravy.

Butter Chicken Murgh Makhani is a celebration of flavors and textures, a dish that embodies the warmth and richness of Indian cuisine. With this detailed guide, you're now equipped to recreate this iconic Indian lunch recipe at home, promising a culinary journey that will delight your senses and impress your loved ones. So gather your ingredients, follow these steps, and prepare to indulge in the magic of homemade Butter Chicken!
